title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
slides: How did a notetaker pass $100M ARR so fast?
Plaid shipped over 2 million AI devices already.
Your team could bypass hardware with just software.
Test today: use their API for your own notes.
thread_text:
1. How did it hit $100M?

A notetaking app scaled faster than most unicorns—without building a single hardware prototype.

2. Plaid already shipped 2M+ AI devices.

But the revenue surge came from software subscriptions, not selling microphones or chips.

3. Software alone drives the margin.

Your team could copy this playbook: skip hardware entirely and use their API for note transcription.

4. No factory, no inventory, no delay.

Pure software lets you iterate weekly and deploy features without supply chain risk.

5. Test it today in 10 minutes.

Grab their API key, send an audio sample, and see if the output beats your current internal tool.
video_script: A notetaker hit 100 million revenue fast.

Software drove most revenue, not the hardware.
You can skip hardware using only software.
Test their API today for your notes.

script: A notetaker hit 100 million revenue fast.
Plaid shipped two million AI devices already.
Software drove most revenue, not the hardware.
You can skip hardware using only software.
Test their API today for your notes.

instagram.caption:
2 million AI notetakers shipped, and the $100M ARR came from software subscriptions—not the hardware itself. 🎙️

The company proved the bulk of the revenue stream is the AI transcript layer, not the device you hold. You can skip building a microphone entirely.

For teams, this means you can copy the model today: deploy a note-taking feature without inventory or factory delays.

Grab their API key, send a 30-second audio clip, and see if the output beats your current internal tool? 🛠️

carousel_slides.description: Software subscriptions drove the surge, not hardware—proving you can scale fast without a single prototype.
Over 2 million AI notetakers sold, yet most revenue came from subscriptions, not microphones or chips.
Software alone drives high margins—no factory, no inventory, just pure code you can iterate weekly.
Your team can copy this playbook: use Plaud's API for note transcription without building a single device.
Grab their API key, send an audio sample, and see if it beats your current internal tool.
